{"type":"step_start","timestamp":1777269442324,"sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","part":{"id":"prt_dcd83870d001hQRYaHwBHPxdH8","messageID":"msg_dcd8381ce001RZa62j0wbi9Y33","sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","type":"step-start"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1777269445288,"sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"skill","callID":"call_00_Y7pWBgnSZSLpO5njyVpivIDz","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"name":"dogfood"},"output":"<skill_content name=\"dogfood\">\n# Skill: dogfood\n\n# Dogfood\n\nSystematically explore a web application, find issues, and produce a report with full reproduction evidence for every finding.\n\n## Setup\n\nOnly the **Target URL** is required. Everything else has sensible defaults -- use them unless the user explicitly provides an override.\n\n| Parameter | Default | Example override |\n|-----------|---------|-----------------|\n| **Target URL** | _(required)_ | `vercel.com`, `http://localhost:3000` |\n| **Session name** | Slugified domain (e.g., `vercel.com` -> `vercel-com`) | `--session my-session` |\n| **Output directory** | `./dogfood-output/` | `Output directory: /tmp/qa` |\n| **Scope** | Full app | `Focus on the billing page` |\n| **Authentication** | None | `Sign in to user@example.com` |\n\nIf the user says something like \"dogfood vercel.com\", start immediately with defaults. Do not ask clarifying questions unless authentication is mentioned but credentials are missing.\n\nAlways use `agent-browser` directly -- never `npx agent-browser`. The direct binary uses the fast Rust client. `npx` routes through Node.js and is significantly slower.\n\n## Workflow\n\n```\n1. Initialize    Set up session, output dirs, report file\n2. Authenticate  Sign in if needed, save state\n3. Orient        Navigate to starting point, take initial snapshot\n4. Explore       Systematically visit pages and test features\n5. Document      Screenshot + record each issue as found\n6. Wrap up       Update summary counts, close session\n```\n\n### 1. Initialize\n\n```bash\nmkdir -p {OUTPUT_DIR}/screenshots {OUTPUT_DIR}/videos\n```\n\nCopy the report template into the output directory and fill in the header fields:\n\n```bash\ncp {SKILL_DIR}/templates/dogfood-report-template.md {OUTPUT_DIR}/report.md\n```\n\nStart a named session:\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} open {TARGET_URL}\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} wait --load networkidle\n```\n\n### 2. Authenticate\n\nIf the app requires login:\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} snapshot -i\n# Identify login form refs, fill credentials\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} fill @e1 \"{EMAIL}\"\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} fill @e2 \"{PASSWORD}\"\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} click @e3\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} wait --load networkidle\n```\n\nFor OTP/email codes: ask the user, wait for their response, then enter the code.\n\nAfter successful login, save state for potential reuse:\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} state save {OUTPUT_DIR}/auth-state.json\n```\n\n### 3. Orient\n\nTake an initial annotated screenshot and snapshot to understand the app structure:\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} screenshot --annotate {OUTPUT_DIR}/screenshots/initial.png\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} snapshot -i\n```\n\nIdentify the main navigation elements and map out the sections to visit.\n\n### 4. Explore\n\nRead [references/issue-taxonomy.md](references/issue-taxonomy.md) for the full list of what to look for and the exploration checklist.\n\n**Strategy -- work through the app systematically:**\n\n- Start from the main navigation. Visit each top-level section.\n- Within each section, test interactive elements: click buttons, fill forms, open dropdowns/modals.\n- Check edge cases: empty states, error handling, boundary inputs.\n- Try realistic end-to-end workflows (create, edit, delete flows).\n- Check the browser console for errors periodically.\n\n**At each page:**\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} snapshot -i\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} screenshot --annotate {OUTPUT_DIR}/screenshots/{page-name}.png\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} errors\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} console\n```\n\nUse your judgment on how deep to go. Spend more time on core features and less on peripheral pages. If you find a cluster of issues in one area, investigate deeper.\n\n### 5. Document Issues (Repro-First)\n\nSteps 4 and 5 happen together -- explore and document in a single pass. When you find an issue, stop exploring and document it immediately before moving on. Do not explore the whole app first and document later.\n\nEvery issue must be reproducible. When you find something wrong, do not just note it -- prove it with evidence. The goal is that someone reading the report can see exactly what happened and replay it.\n\n**Choose the right level of evidence for the issue:**\n\n#### Interactive / behavioral issues (functional, ux, console errors on action)\n\nThese require user interaction to reproduce -- use full repro with video and step-by-step screenshots:\n\n1. **Start a repro video** _before_ reproducing:\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} record start {OUTPUT_DIR}/videos/issue-{NNN}-repro.webm\n```\n\n2. **Walk through the steps at human pace.** Pause 1-2 seconds between actions so the video is watchable. Take a screenshot at each step:\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} screenshot {OUTPUT_DIR}/screenshots/issue-{NNN}-step-1.png\nsleep 1\n# Perform action (click, fill, etc.)\nsleep 1\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} screenshot {OUTPUT_DIR}/screenshots/issue-{NNN}-step-2.png\nsleep 1\n# ...continue until the issue manifests\n```\n\n3. **Capture the broken state.** Pause so the viewer can see it, then take an annotated screenshot:\n\n```bash\nsleep 2\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} screenshot --annotate {OUTPUT_DIR}/screenshots/issue-{NNN}-result.png\n```\n\n4. **Stop the video:**\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} record stop\n```\n\n5. Write numbered repro steps in the report, each referencing its screenshot.\n\n#### Static / visible-on-load issues (typos, placeholder text, clipped text, misalignment, console errors on load)\n\nThese are visible without interaction -- a single annotated screenshot is sufficient. No video, no multi-step repro:\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} screenshot --annotate {OUTPUT_DIR}/screenshots/issue-{NNN}.png\n```\n\nWrite a brief description and reference the screenshot in the report. Set **Repro Video** to `N/A`.\n\n---\n\n**For all issues:**\n\n1. **Append to the report immediately.** Do not batch issues for later. Write each one as you find it so nothing is lost if the session is interrupted.\n\n2. **Increment the issue counter** (ISSUE-001, ISSUE-002, ...).\n\n### 6. Wrap Up\n\nAim to find **5-10 well-documented issues**, then wrap up. Depth of evidence matters more than total count -- 5 issues with full repro beats 20 with vague descriptions.\n\nAfter exploring:\n\n1. Re-read the report and update the summary severity counts so they match the actual issues. Every `### ISSUE-` block must be reflected in the totals.\n2. Close the session:\n\n```bash\nagent-browser --session {SESSION} close\n```\n\n3. Tell the user the report is ready and summarize findings: total issues, breakdown by severity, and the most critical items.\n\n## Guidance\n\n- **Repro is everything.** Every issue needs proof -- but match the evidence to the issue. Interactive bugs need video and step-by-step screenshots. Static bugs (typos, placeholder text, visual glitches visible on load) only need a single annotated screenshot.\n- **Don't record video for static issues.** A typo or clipped text doesn't benefit from a video. Save video for issues that involve user interaction, timing, or state changes.\n- **For interactive issues, screenshot each step.** Capture the before, the action, and the after -- so someone can see the full sequence.\n- **Write repro steps that map to screenshots.** Each numbered step in the report should reference its corresponding screenshot. A reader should be able to follow the steps visually without touching a browser.\n- **Be thorough but use judgment.** You are not following a test script -- you are exploring like a real user would. If something feels off, investigate.\n- **Write findings incrementally.** Append each issue to the report as you discover it. If the session is interrupted, findings are preserved. Never batch all issues for the end.\n- **Never delete output files.** Do not `rm` screenshots, videos, or the report mid-session. Do not close the session and restart. Work forward, not backward.\n- **Never read the target app's source code.** You are testing as a user, not auditing code. Do not read HTML, JS, or config files of the app under test. All findings must come from what you observe in the browser.\n- **Check the console.** Many issues are invisible in the UI but show up as JS errors or failed requests.\n- **Test like a user, not a robot.** Try common workflows end-to-end. Click things a real user would click. Enter realistic data.\n- **Type like a human.** When filling form fields during video recording, use `type` instead of `fill` -- it types character-by-character. Use `fill` only outside of video recording when speed matters.\n- **Pace repro videos for humans.** Add `sleep 1` between actions and `sleep 2` before the final result screenshot. Videos should be watchable at 1x speed -- a human reviewing the report needs to see what happened, not a blur of instant state changes.\n- **Be efficient with commands.** Batch multiple `agent-browser` commands in a single shell call when they are independent (e.g., `agent-browser ... screenshot ... && agent-browser ... console`). Use `agent-browser --session {SESSION} scroll down 300` for scrolling -- do not use `key` or `evaluate` to scroll.\n\n## References\n\n| Reference | When to Read |\n|-----------|--------------|\n| [references/issue-taxonomy.md](references/issue-taxonomy.md) | Start of session -- calibrate what to look for, severity levels, exploration checklist |\n\n## Templates\n\n| Template | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|\n| [templates/dogfood-report-template.md](templates/dogfood-report-template.md) | Copy into output directory as the report file |\n\nBase directory for this skill: file:///home/the_bomb/.agents/skills/dogfood\nRelative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.\nNote: file list is sampled.\n\n<skill_files>\n<file>/home/the_bomb/.agents/skills/dogfood/templates/dogfood-report-template.md</file>\n<file>/home/the_bomb/.agents/skills/dogfood/references/issue-taxonomy.md</file>\n</skill_files>\n</skill_content>","metadata":{"name":"dogfood","dir":"/home/the_bomb/.agents/skills/dogfood","truncated":false},"title":"Loaded skill: dogfood","time":{"start":1777269445282,"end":1777269445287}},"id":"prt_dcd8390b8001NaMtDpg3LgpXoE","sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","messageID":"msg_dcd8381ce001RZa62j0wbi9Y33"}}
{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1777269446470,"sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_01_A5QkJVBwCaqowCsODKsPxmpA","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/home/the_bomb/orkes_ds/dogfood-output/report.md"},"output":"<path>/home/the_bomb/orkes_ds/dogfood-output/report.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Dogfood Report: 草東沒有派對 Lyrics Page\n2: \n3: | Field | Value |\n4: |-------|-------|\n5: | **Date** | 2026-04-27 |\n6: | **App URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n7: | **Session** | caodong-lyrics |\n8: | **Scope** | Full single-page app: lyrics display, responsiveness, visual quality, content accuracy, accessibility |\n9: \n10: ## Summary\n11: \n12: | Severity | Count |\n13: |----------|-------|\n14: | Critical | 0 |\n15: | High | 0 |\n16: | Medium | 3 |\n17: | Low | 5 |\n18: | **Total** | **8** |\n19: \n20: ## Issues\n21: \n22: ### ISSUE-001: Pinyin \"Oh\" incorrectly split into \"O h\" with extra spaces\n23: \n24: | Field | Value |\n25: |-------|-------|\n26: | **Severity** | medium |\n27: | **Category** | content |\n28: | **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n29: | **Repro Video** | N/A |\n30: \n31: **Description**\n32: \n33: The pinyin column splits the English interjection \"Oh\" into \"O h\" with a space between O and h. The pinyin generation system treated \"Oh\" as if it were pinyin syllables rather than an English word, producing unreadable output across multiple tracks. 9 instances found across Tracks 3 and 4.\n34: \n35: **Evidence (via JS eval)**\n36: \n37: ```\n38: Track 3 - 爛泥 (Wimpish):\n39:   O h , duō me měi lì de yī kē xīn     ← should be \"Oh, duō me...\"\n40:   O h , duō me dān chún de yī shǒu shī\n41: \n42: Track 4 - 勇敢的人 (The Reluctant):\n43:   O h , méi yǒu yǒng gǎn de rén\n44:   O h , tā men rēng le nǐ de shì jiè\n45: ```\n46: \n47: **Repro Steps**\n48: \n49: 1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n50: 2. Scroll to Track 3 \"爛泥 / Wimpish\" or Track 4 \"勇敢的人 / The Reluctant\"\n51: 3. **Observe:** In the pinyin (green) column, \"Oh\" is rendered as \"O h\" with a space splitting the letters.\n52:    ![Result](screenshots/issue-001-oh-bug.png)\n53: \n54: ---\n55: \n56: ### ISSUE-002: Pinyin \"Oo\" incorrectly split into \"O o\" with extra spaces\n57: \n58: | Field | Value |\n59: |-------|-------|\n60: | **Severity** | medium |\n61: | **Category** | content |\n62: | **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n63: | **Repro Video** | N/A |\n64: \n65: **Description**\n66: \n67: Same root cause as ISSUE-001. The vocalization \"Oo-oo-ooh\" in Track 11 is split into \"O o - o o - o o h\" — every character separated by spaces. 4 instances found.\n68: \n69: **Evidence (via JS eval)**\n70: \n71: ```\n72: Track 11 - 我們 (Us):\n73:   O o - o o - o o h ,   o o - o o h … （ zhǎo jiā ）\n74:   O o - o o - o o h ,   o o - o o h … （ zhǎo ）\n75: ```\n76: \n77: **Repro Steps**\n78: \n79: 1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n80: 2. Scroll to Track 11 \"我們 / Us\"\n81: 3. **Observe:** In the pinyin column, \"Oo-oo-ooh\" vocalizations appear as \"O o - o o - o o h\".\n82: \n83: ---\n84: \n85: ### ISSUE-003: No focus indicators on interactive elements\n86: \n87: | Field | Value |\n88: |-------|-------|\n89: | **Severity** | medium |\n90: | **Category** | accessibility |\n91: | **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n92: | **Repro Video** | N/A |\n93: \n94: **Description**\n95: \n96: All 4 navigation links (`丑奴儿`, `瓦合`, `About`, `↑ back-to-top`) have no visible focus indicator. `getComputedStyle` confirms `outlineStyle: \"none\"` with no alternative focus styling. Keyboard-only users cannot determine which element is currently focused. Violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).\n97: \n98: **Evidence**\n99: \n100: ```json\n101: {\"focusVisible\": false, \"navSticky\": \"fixed\", \"linkColor\": \"rgb(136, 136, 136)\"}\n102: ```\n103: \n104: **Repro Steps**\n105: \n106: 1. Open https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n107: 2. Press Tab to navigate through links\n108: 3. **Observe:** No visual indicator (outline, ring, background change) appears on focused links.\n109: \n110: ---\n111: \n112: ### ISSUE-004: CJK/fullwidth punctuation in pinyin column\n113: \n114: | Field | Value |\n115: |-------|-------|\n116: | **Severity** | low |\n117: | **Category** | content |\n118: | **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n119: | **Repro Video** | N/A |\n120: \n121: **Description**\n122: \n123: Throughout the entire page, the pinyin column uses Chinese/fullwidth punctuation marks instead of standard ASCII punctuation. Pinyin romanization conventions typically use ASCII punctuation. The Chinese punctuation appears throughout all 20 non-instrumental tracks.\n124: \n125: **Examples of CJK punctuation used in pinyin:**\n126: - `，` (U+FF0C) — Chinese comma instead of ASCII `,`\n127: - `？` (U+FF1F) — Chinese question mark instead of ASCII `?`\n128: - `、` (U+3001) — enumeration comma instead of ASCII `,`\n129: - `：` (U+FF1A) — Chinese colon\n130: - `（）` (U+FF08/FF09) — Chinese parentheses\n131: \n132: **Repro Steps**\n133: \n134: 1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n135: 2. Scroll to any track with lyrics\n136: 3. **Observe:** Punctuation in the pinyin column uses fullwidth CJK characters.\n137: \n138: ---\n139: \n140: ### ISSUE-005: Song titles are `<span>` elements, not semantic headings\n141: \n142: | Field | Value |\n143: |-------|-------|\n144: | **Severity** | low |\n145: | **Category** | accessibility |\n146: | **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n147: | **Repro Video** | N/A |\n148: \n149: **Description**\n150: \n151: All 23 song titles (`.song-title`) and their English counterparts (`.song-title-en`) are `<span>` elements instead of heading elements (`<h3>` or `<h4>`). Screen reader users cannot navigate between tracks using heading shortcuts. The page has only 1 `<h1>` and 2 `<h2>` elements despite containing 23 track sections.\n152: \n153: **Evidence (via eval)**\n154: \n155: ```json\n156: {\"song-title\": {\"tag\": \"SPAN\"}, \"song-title-en\": {\"tag\": \"SPAN\"}}\n157: ```\n158: \n159: **Repro Steps**\n160: \n161: 1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n162: 2. Open a screen reader or inspect the DOM\n163: 3. **Observe:** Song titles are `<span>` elements without heading semantics.\n164: \n165: ---\n166: \n167: ### ISSUE-006: Missing meta description and social sharing tags\n168: \n169: | Field | Value |\n170: |-------|-------|\n171: | **Severity** | low |\n172: | **Category** | content |\n173: | **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n174: | **Repro Video** | N/A |\n175: \n176: **Description**\n177: \n178: The page is missing all social/sharing metadata tags. When shared on social media (Twitter, Facebook, Discord, Slack), no preview card will be generated. Missing:\n179: - `<meta name=\"description\">`\n180: - `<meta property=\"og:title\">` / `<meta property=\"og:description\">` / `<meta property=\"og:image\">`\n181: - `<meta name=\"twitter:card\">`\n182: \n183: **Repro Steps**\n184: \n185: 1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n186: 2. View page source or use `document.querySelector(\"meta[name=description]\")`\n187: 3. **Observe:** No description or Open Graph tags present.\n188: \n189: ---\n190: \n191: ### ISSUE-007: Missing `<main>` landmark element\n192: \n193: | Field | Value |\n194: |-------|-------|\n195: | **Severity** | low |\n196: | **Category** | accessibility |\n197: | **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n198: | **Repro Video** | N/A |\n199: \n200: **Description**\n201: \n202: The content area is not wrapped in a `<main>` element. Screen readers use landmarks to skip navigation and jump directly to content. The page has `<nav>` for the header links but the body content itself lacks a `<main>` wrapper. This is a minor structural issue.\n203: \n204: **Evidence**\n205: \n206: ```json\n207: {\"hasMain\": false, \"hasNav\": true}\n208: ```\n209: \n210: **Repro Steps**\n211: \n212: 1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n213: 2. The browser tab and bookmarks will show a generic document icon instead of a branded icon. Look at the browser tab\n235: 3. **Observe:** Generic/default favicon displayed.\n236: \n237: ---\n238: \n239: ## Positive Findings\n240: \n241: - **Zero JavaScript errors** — console clean across desktop (1280x900), tablet (768x1024), and mobile (375x812) viewports\n242: - **Zero failed network requests** — static HTML page, all CSS inlined\n243: - **No broken links** — all 4 nav items (丑奴儿, 瓦合, About, ↑) resolve to valid anchor targets\n244: - **Responsive layout works** — 3-column grid → 2-column at <900px → single column at <700px\n245: - **Good color contrast** — body text (#CCC) on background (#0A0A0A) has ~12:1 ratio (WCAG AAA)\n246: - **lang=\"zh-TW\"** — proper document language attribute set\n247: - **Correct viewport meta** — `width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0`\n248: - **All 23 tracks present** — 12 from 醜奴兒 + 11 from 瓦合, matching the source albums\n249: - **No overflow/truncation issues** — all content fits within the layout at tested viewports\n250: - **No external dependencies** — pure HTML+CSS with no JS dependency\n\n(End of file - total 250 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Dogfood Report: 草東沒有派對 Lyrics Page\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Date** | 2026-04-27 |\n| **App URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n| **Session** | caodong-lyrics |\n| **Scope** | Full single-page app: lyrics display, responsiveness, visual quality, content accuracy, accessibility |\n\n## Summary\n\n| Severity | Count |\n|----------|-------|\n| Critical | 0 |\n| High | 0 |\n| Medium | 3 |\n| Low | 5 |\n| **Total** | **8** |\n\n## Issues","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"home/the_bomb/orkes_ds/dogfood-output/report.md","time":{"start":1777269446466,"end":1777269446469}},"id":"prt_dcd8393a3001mWjUa2t7UMSKlD","sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","messageID":"msg_dcd8381ce001RZa62j0wbi9Y33"}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1777269453938,"sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"read","callID":"call_00_ZcYhoWPoSMqaOuZxjgyIS3Qv","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/home/the_bomb/.agents/skills/dogfood/references/issue-taxonomy.md"},"output":"<path>/home/the_bomb/.agents/skills/dogfood/references/issue-taxonomy.md</path>\n<type>file</type>\n<content>\n1: # Issue Taxonomy\n2: \n3: Reference for categorizing issues found during dogfooding. Read this at the start of a dogfood session to calibrate what to look for.\n4: \n5: ## Contents\n6: \n7: - [Severity Levels](#severity-levels)\n8: - [Categories](#categories)\n9: - [Exploration Checklist](#exploration-checklist)\n10: \n11: ## Severity Levels\n12: \n13: | Severity | Definition |\n14: |----------|------------|\n15: | **critical** | Blocks a core workflow, causes data loss, or crashes the app |\n16: | **high** | Major feature broken or unusable, no workaround |\n17: | **medium** | Feature works but with noticeable problems, workaround exists |\n18: | **low** | Minor cosmetic or polish issue |\n19: \n20: ## Categories\n21: \n22: ### Visual / UI\n23: \n24: - Layout broken or misaligned elements\n25: - Overlapping or clipped text\n26: - Inconsistent spacing, padding, or margins\n27: - Missing or broken icons/images\n28: - Dark mode / light mode rendering issues\n29: - Responsive layout problems (viewport sizes)\n30: - Z-index stacking issues (elements hidden behind others)\n31: - Font rendering issues (wrong font, size, weight)\n32: - Color contrast problems\n33: - Animation glitches or jank\n34: \n35: ### Functional\n36: \n37: - Broken links (404, wrong destination)\n38: - Buttons or controls that do nothing on click\n39: - Form validation that rejects valid input or accepts invalid input\n40: - Incorrect redirects\n41: - Features that fail silently\n42: - State not persisted when expected (lost on refresh, navigation)\n43: - Race conditions (double-submit, stale data)\n44: - Broken search or filtering\n45: - Pagination issues\n46: - File upload/download failures\n47: \n48: ### UX\n49: \n50: - Confusing or unclear navigation\n51: - Missing loading indicators or feedback after actions\n52: - Slow or unresponsive interactions (>300ms perceived delay)\n53: - Unclear error messages\n54: - Missing confirmation for destructive actions\n55: - Dead ends (no way to go back or proceed)\n56: - Inconsistent patterns across similar features\n57: - Missing keyboard shortcuts or focus management\n58: - Unintuitive defaults\n59: - Missing empty states or unhelpful empty states\n60: \n61: ### Content\n62: \n63: - Typos or grammatical errors\n64: - Outdated or incorrect text\n65: - Placeholder or lorem ipsum content left in\n66: - Truncated text without tooltip or expansion\n67: - Missing or wrong labels\n68: - Inconsistent terminology\n69: \n70: ### Performance\n71: \n72: - Slow page loads (>3s)\n73: - Janky scrolling or animations\n74: - Large layout shifts (content jumping)\n75: - Excessive network requests (check via console/network)\n76: - Memory leaks (page slows over time)\n77: - Unoptimized images (large file sizes)\n78: \n79: ### Console / Errors\n80: \n81: - JavaScript exceptions in console\n82: - Failed network requests (4xx, 5xx)\n83: - Deprecation warnings\n84: - CORS errors\n85: - Mixed content warnings\n86: - Unhandled promise rejections\n87: \n88: ### Accessibility\n89: \n90: - Missing alt text on images\n91: - Unlabeled form inputs\n92: - Poor keyboard navigation (can't tab to elements)\n93: - Focus traps\n94: - Insufficient color contrast\n95: - Missing ARIA attributes on dynamic content\n96: - Screen reader incompatible patterns\n97: \n98: ## Exploration Checklist\n99: \n100: Use this as a guide for what to test on each page/feature:\n101: \n102: 1. **Visual scan** -- Take an annotated screenshot. Look for layout, alignment, and rendering issues.\n103: 2. **Interactive elements** -- Click every button, link, and control. Do they work? Is there feedback?\n104: 3. **Forms** -- Fill and submit. Test empty submission, invalid input, and edge cases.\n105: 4. **Navigation** -- Follow all navigation paths. Check breadcrumbs, back button, deep links.\n106: 5. **States** -- Check empty states, loading states, error states, and full/overflow states.\n107: 6. **Console** -- Check for JS errors, failed requests, and warnings.\n108: 7. **Responsiveness** -- If relevant, test at different viewport sizes.\n109: 8. **Auth boundaries** -- Test what happens when not logged in, with different roles if applicable.\n\n(End of file - total 109 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Issue Taxonomy\n\nReference for categorizing issues found during dogfooding. Read this at the start of a dogfood session to calibrate what to look for.\n\n## Contents\n\n- [Severity Levels](#severity-levels)\n- [Categories](#categories)\n- [Exploration Checklist](#exploration-checklist)\n\n## Severity Levels\n\n| Severity | Definition |\n|----------|------------|\n| **critical** | Blocks a core workflow, causes data loss, or crashes the app |\n| **high** | Major feature broken or unusable, no workaround |\n| **medium** | Feature works but with noticeable problems, workaround exists |\n| **low** | Minor cosmetic or polish issue |\n\n## Categories","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"home/the_bomb/.agents/skills/dogfood/references/issue-taxonomy.md","time":{"start":1777269453934,"end":1777269453937}},"id":"prt_dcd83b16e001uRkK0aTmysXSmH","sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","messageID":"msg_dcd83984b001ROMWUB0HMUDcsL"}}

{"type":"tool_use","timestamp":1777269858486,"sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","part":{"type":"tool","tool":"write","callID":"call_00_A5LDvSTusc1WMXWeSqCgLk0o","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"filePath":"/home/the_bomb/orkes_ds/dogfood-output/report.md","content":"# Dogfood Report: 草東沒有派對 Lyrics Page (v2)\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Date** | 2026-04-27 |\n| **App URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n| **Session** | caodong-v2 |\n| **Scope** | Full single-page app: lyrics display, responsiveness, visual quality, content accuracy, accessibility |\n\n## Summary\n\n| Severity | Count |\n|----------|-------|\n| Critical | 0 |\n| High | 0 |\n| Medium | 2 |\n| Low | 5 |\n| **Total** | **7** |\n\n## Previously Fixed Since Last Pass\n\n| Old Issue | Status |\n|-----------|--------|\n| ISSUE-001 (Oh split into \"O h\") | **FIXED** — 0 occurrences. \"Oh\" now renders correctly throughout. |\n| ISSUE-004 (CJK punctuation in pinyin) | **FIXED** — No CJK punctuation detected in pinyin column. |\n\n## Issues\n\n### ISSUE-001: Pinyin \"Oo\" vocalization split into \"O o\" with spaces\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Severity** | medium |\n| **Category** | content |\n| **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/#clod |\n| **Repro Video** | N/A |\n\n**Description**\n\nThe pinyin generation system treats the vocalization \"Oo\" (as in \"oo-oo-ooh\") as pinyin syllables and splits it into \"O o\". 4 instances found in Track 11 (我們 / Us).\n\n**Evidence (via document.body.innerText)**\n\n```\nO o - o o - o o h ,   o o - o o h … （ zhǎo jiā ）\nO o - o o - o o h ,   o o - o o h … （ zhǎo ）\nO o - o o - o o h ,   o o - o o h … （ yòng jǐn yī qiè huàn lái de zhǐ zhāng ）\nO o - o o - o o h ,   o o - o o h … （ yòng jǐn yī qiè ！ ）\n```\n\nExpected: `Oo-oo-ooh` / `Oo-oo-ooh` (each vocalization as a single word without spaces between O and o).\n\n**Repro Steps**\n\n1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n2. Click \"瓦合 · The Clod\" in the nav\n3. Scroll down to Track 11 \"我們 / Us\"\n4. **Observe:** In the pinyin (green) column, \"Oo\" is rendered as \"O o\" with a space between O and o.\n   ![Result](screenshots/issue-001-oo-bug.png)\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE-002: Focus outline invisible due to insufficient color contrast\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Severity** | medium |\n| **Category** | accessibility |\n| **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n| **Repro Video** | videos/issue-002-focus.webm |\n\n**Description**\n\nThe browser default focus outline renders at `rgb(16, 16, 16)` on a background of `rgb(10, 10, 10)` — a contrast ratio of approximately 1.03:1, making the focus ring **invisible to sighted keyboard users**. The outline technically exists (`outlineStyle: \"auto\"`, `outlineWidth: \"1px\"`) but cannot be seen because the color is near-identical to the dark background. Violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) and WCAG 1.4.3 (Contrast Minimum — focus indicator needs ≥3:1 against background).\n\nAll 4 nav links affected: 丑奴儿, 瓦合, About, ↑.\n\n**Evidence (via getComputedStyle on focused element)**\n\n```json\n{\n  \"outline\": \"rgb(16, 16, 16) auto 1px\",\n  \"outlineStyle\": \"auto\",\n  \"outlineWidth\": \"1px\",\n  \"outlineColor\": \"rgb(16, 16, 16)\",\n  \"bodyBackground\": \"rgb(10, 10, 10)\"\n}\n```\n\n**Repro Steps**\n\n1. [Step 1] Open https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n   ![Step 1](screenshots/issue-002-step-1.png)\n2. [Step 2] Press Tab to focus the first nav link (\"丑奴儿 · The Servile\")\n   ![Step 2](screenshots/issue-002-step-2.png)\n3. [Step 3] Press Tab again to focus the second nav link (\"瓦合 · The Clod\")\n   ![Step 3](screenshots/issue-002-step-3.png)\n4. **Observe:** No visible focus indicator on any link. The outline exists but is indistinguishable from the background.\n   ![Result](screenshots/issue-002-result.png)\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE-003: Back-to-top button links to #about instead of page top\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Severity** | low |\n| **Category** | functional |\n| **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n| **Repro Video** | N/A |\n\n**Description**\n\nThe `↑` back-to-top button has `href=\"#about\"` instead of a proper back-to-top target (e.g. `href=\"#\"`, `href=\"#top\"`, or a JS scroll-to-top). It works only because the About section happens to be at the top of the page (`scrollY: 0`), but this is semantically incorrect and breaks if the About section ever moves.\n\n**Evidence (via document.querySelector)**\n\n```json\n{\"text\": \"↑\", \"href\": \"#about\", \"classes\": \"back-to-top\"}\n```\n\nElement `id=\"top\"` does not exist in the DOM.\n\n**Repro Steps**\n\n1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n2. Scroll halfway down the page\n3. Click the `↑` button in the nav bar\n4. **Observe:** Browser navigates to `#about` hash. Functionally goes to top (since About is at Y=0), but semantically incorrect.\n   ![Result](screenshots/issue-003-backtotop.png)\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE-004: Song titles are `<span>` elements, not semantic headings\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Severity** | low |\n| **Category** | accessibility |\n| **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n| **Repro Video** | N/A |\n\n**Description**\n\nAll 46 song title elements (`.song-title`, `.song-title-en`) across 23 tracks are `<span>` elements instead of heading elements (`<h3>` or `<h4>`). Screen reader users cannot navigate between tracks using heading landmarks. The page has only 1 `<h1>` and 2 `<h2>` elements.\n\n**Evidence**\n\n```json\n{\n  \"h1Count\": 1,\n  \"h2Count\": 2,\n  \"h3Count\": 0,\n  \"h4Count\": 0,\n  \"songTitleTags\": [\"SPAN\", \"SPAN\", \"SPAN\"],\n  \"totalSongTitleSpans\": 46\n}\n```\n\n**Repro Steps**\n\n1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n2. Open a screen reader or inspect the DOM\n3. **Observe:** Song titles are `<span>` elements. No `<h3>` or `<h4>` tags present.\n   ![Result](screenshots/issue-004-span-headings.png)\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE-005: Missing meta description and social sharing tags\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Severity** | low |\n| **Category** | content |\n| **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n| **Repro Video** | N/A |\n\n**Description**\n\nThe page is missing all social/sharing metadata tags. When shared on social media (Twitter, Facebook, Discord, Slack), no preview card is generated. Missing: `<meta name=\"description\">`, `og:title/description/image`, `twitter:card`.\n\n**Evidence**\n\n```json\n{\"metaDesc\": false, \"ogCount\": 0, \"favicon\": false}\n```\n\n**Repro Steps**\n\n1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n2. View page source or check `<meta>` tags\n3. **Observe:** No description or Open Graph tags present.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE-006: Missing `<main>` landmark element\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Severity** | low |\n| **Category** | accessibility |\n| **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n| **Repro Video** | N/A |\n\n**Description**\n\nThe content area is not wrapped in a `<main>` element. Screen readers use landmarks to skip navigation and jump directly to content. The page has `<nav>` for the header links but main content is a direct child of `<body>`.\n\n**Evidence**\n\n```json\n{\"hasMain\": false, \"hasNav\": true}\n```\n\n**Repro Steps**\n\n1. Navigate to https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/\n2. Inspect the DOM structure\n3. **Observe:** No `<main>` tag; content flows directly under `<body>`.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE-007: Missing favicon\n\n| Field | Value |\n|-------|-------|\n| **Severity** | low |\n| **Category** | visual |\n| **URL** | https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ |\n| **Repro Video** | N/A |\n\n**Description**\n\nNo favicon is defined. The browser tab and bookmarks show a generic document icon instead of a branded icon. Both `<link rel=\"icon\">` and `<link rel=\"apple-touch-icon\">` are absent.\n\n**Repro Steps**\n\n1. Open https://nopartyforcaodong.zeidgeist.com/ in a browser\n2. Look at the browser tab\n3. **Observe:** Generic/default favicon displayed.\n\n---\n\n## Positive Findings\n\n- **Zero JavaScript errors** — console clean at 1280x900, 768x1024, and 375x812\n- **Zero failed network requests** — static HTML page, all CSS inlined\n- **No broken links** — all 3 nav items + ↑ resolve correctly (丑奴儿→#servile, 瓦合→#clod, About→#about, ↑→page top)\n- **Responsive layout works** — 3-column grid → 2-column at <900px → single column at <700px\n- **No horizontal overflow** — body width matches viewport at all tested sizes\n- **Good text color contrast** — body text `#CCC` on background `#0A0A0A` has ~12:1 ratio (WCAG AAA)\n- **`lang=\"zh-TW\"`** — proper document language attribute set\n- **Correct viewport meta** — `width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0`\n- **All 23 tracks present** — 12 from 醜奴兒 + 11 from 瓦合, matching the source albums\n- **Sticky nav works** — fixed positioning at top with z-index 100\n- **No external JS dependencies** — single inline script, pure HTML+CSS\n- **2 previously reported issues fixed** — Oh split bug and CJK punctuation both resolved\n- **Pinyin column responsive** — English column hidden below 700px, pinyin hidden below 500px (verified at 375x812)\n"},"output":"Wrote file successfully.","metadata":{"diagnostics":{},"filepath":"/home/the_bomb/orkes_ds/dogfood-output/report.md","exists":true,"truncated":false},"title":"home/the_bomb/orkes_ds/dogfood-output/report.md","time":{"start":1777269858476,"end":1777269858485}},"id":"prt_dcd88d381001G1gjcR06BgdDC6","sessionID":"ses_2327cb677ffe2DzbKsLxmzW1vp","messageID":"msg_dcd88b34d001xiDKNYNOZQWaJY"}}
